rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA S. VILLANUEVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether to take the matter off calendar as the following procedural issues remain unaddressed: 1) Notice of Hearing (Form DE-120) is required for this proceeding and Box 1 must be checked. (Probate Code Section 12201). 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) The filing of a report of status of administration shall include a petition requesting additional time for estate administration. For the Court to make an order, the report and petition must include a prayer specifying the relief sought (LRSF 14.35.J.2). 4) The instant petition requests that the court schedule a hearing on the amended second and final report and account of exectuor. In order for the court to give a hearing date, a distribution petition must be filed. 5) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) A correct and complete notice of irrevocability of the trust has never been filed. [Probate Code Sections 16061.7(f)-(h).] 2) The successor trustee's verification of this fourth amended accounting petition was dated 8/24/23. 3) The Schwab statements appear to be out of order and do not include a statement verifying the ending balances on 12/31/23. 4) Without the second (and final) accounting in the related conservatorship proceeding, which accounting period should overlap with this one, the accuracy of this accounting cannot be confirmed. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Transfer Of Possession Of Personal Property Belonging To The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/6/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received yet by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) A supplement was filed on 3/11/26, but no notice appears to have been given of the supplement. [CRC 7.53(b).] (3) The proof of service filed on 4/23/26 shows that notice was mailed to Angela Rockett at an address that differs from the one shown in the petition and notice was given to James Rocket, Jr., who is deceased. (4) It is still unclear who all the interested parties are (heirs), who are entitled to personal service of notice if they are not trust beneficiaries. [Probate Code Section 17203(b).] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the second amended petition and all subsequent supporting papers to the assigned examiner by 6/22/26. No courtesy copy of the second amended petition has been received yet by the assigned examiner, so it has not yet been fully reviewed. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MATILDA A. ANDRES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) WHO are the "interested persons" who were recently discovered? What are their relationships to the decedent? (It would be helpful if the family tree were in 12-point type, as required by CRC 2.104 and if a courtesy copy were provided to the examiner, as required by LRSF 14.7.) (2) They were reportedly given notice, but they and their relationships to the decedent, and their addresses were not set forth in a verified supplement to the petition. (3) "Confirmed biological line" is meaningless; the explicit RELATIONSHIP to the decedent must be set forth. (4) If a person was not survived by "biological children" or had no "living biological children," was that person was survived by adopted children? (5) The dates of death (or at least the year if the year preceded the year of Decedent's death) must be set forth for any deceased heirs. (6) The fact that Decedent made gifts to certain heirs during her lifetime does not dismiss those heirs as parties entitled to notice of a petition to administer the decedent's estate. (7) A will that is not self-proving may be proved by a Proof of Subscribing Witness. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/29/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Petitioner might consider hiring an heir finding company instead of a local law firm. Defects should be addressed in a supplement or amendment that stands on its own and simply provides the missing or correct information, not in response to examiner's notes. There is also no need to report that a correct form of notice will be mailed; it should just be mailed and a courtesy copy of the proof of service provided to the examiner. Photos of Facebook pages are not required to be filed; the petitioner's verification is sufficient unless the Court requests supporting documentation.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTY JEAN WILKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether Probate Code Section 6124 does not apply and whether to waive the following procedural issues: 1) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends (CRC 7.53(a)). 2) In Item 5a(2), box (a) or (b) must be checked. 3) Item 6f is checked but Item 6b appears to apply. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/20/26 to prepare matter for hearing

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF PETER H. PANG AND LINDA Y. PANG REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST ORIGINALLY RECORDED: JANUARY 2, 2008 AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions, Accounting, Distribution Of Trust Assets, Removal Of Trustee, Surcharge, And Modification Of Trust Terms (Probate Code 17200, 16060, 16061, 16440, 15409, 15642)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was just e-filed on 4/27/26. Trust petitions require 30 days' notice. (2) No courtesy copy of the amended petition has been received by the assigned examiner as of 5/5/26 so it has not yet been fully reviewed. (3) The only correct form of notice for a trust petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, which must show the full, correct caption of the pleading to be heard. The proof of service is on the reverse side of this form. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the complete, correct Notice of Hearing of the amended petition that was served must be attached to the proof of service. Please provide courtesy copies of the amended petition and proof of service of notice of the amended petition to be heard on 7/13/26 to the assigned examiner by 6/29/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EILEEN MARY REIMONENQ LIVING TRUST DATED JULY 16, 1992
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order For Construction Of Terms Of Trust (Probate Code 17200)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN Y. YIM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The proof of service was incompletely verified. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Please file a copy of the IRA account statement immediately preceding the date of death to confirm how title was held. (The balance may be redacted.) (5) Item 2b of the petition was presumably completed in error if the surviving spouse is still living. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any new papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/29/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BARBARA E. GROTH FAMILY TRUST DATED MAY 27, 2011 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Contest And Invalidate Trust Amendment; For Financial Elder Abuse; And For Orders Restricting Trustee Powers
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/13/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) No notice of hearing (Form DE-120) has been filed with the court (Probate Code Section 17203). 2) The trust instrument and its amendment were not attached to the instant petition, and must be filed with the court.. 3) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LSRF 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 06/29/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUIS PAK YIP
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. DISCLOSURE: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 11/15/2027.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUNKO MAGEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 11/15/27. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEVEN STROPPIANA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. MICHAEL KASOLAS will be appointed probate referee. DISCLOSURE: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 11/29/2027.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HELEN MAY GILES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: court to determine whether petition can be granted given that decedent's death has already been established. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
